question.text: The Prevention of Money-laundering (Maintenance of Records) Rules, 2005 were framed in exercise of powers conferred by which section of PMLA?

explanation.text:
**The Logic:** The PML (Maintenance of Records) Rules, 2005 were framed in exercise of the powers conferred by Section 73 of PMLA, which grants the Central Government the power to make Rules. These rules were made in consultation with the Reserve Bank of India.
**The Trap:** Section 3 defines the offence of ML, Section 12 deals with maintenance of records (the obligation), and Section 56 covers international cooperation. Section 73 is the rule-making power — the enabling provision that authorizes the government to create detailed procedural rules.
**Cross-Exam Context:** Understanding the distinction between an Act (passed by Parliament) and Rules (made by the government under enabling provisions of the Act) is foundational legal knowledge tested in NISM XXIV.
**Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Rules are secondary to the Act and provide flexibility in implementation. The PML Rules were amended in 2023 (Gazette notification dated March 7, 2023) to become the PML (Maintenance of Records) Amendment Rules, 2023.
